description: Evals for the EyeLevel web UI skill. Each test prompt exercises a different surface area (page-level layout, modal/form, settings list). The rubric checks that the generated code follows the canonical tokens, wrappers, and corrections — not just that it compiles. The skill is expected to read ../eyelevel-design-standards/references/ for brand invariants.

tests.prompt: Build an API Keys management page for the GroundX dashboard. It should show a list of the user's existing keys in a table (columns: Label, Key Prefix, Created, Last Used, Actions) with a '+ NEW KEY' pill button in the top-right of the section header. Each row has a copy-to-clipboard icon for the prefix and a delete icon. Include an empty state for when the user has no keys yet. Use the design system.

must_use: GxCard as the outer surface (not MUI Card or inline Box with border sx)
GxSectionHeader for the 'API KEYS' row with the + NEW KEY action on the right
CommonSubmitButton with startIcon={<AddIcon />} and borderRadius: BORDER_RADIUS_PILL for the pill CTA
Named constants from '@/constants' — CORAL, BORDER, BORDER_RADIUS_CARD, PADDING — no hex literals
MUI IconButton with aria-label for copy and delete actions
fontSize='small' on icons inside table rows
Responsive behavior via theme.breakpoints or sx responsive objects
must_not_use: Hardcoded hex colors anywhere in JSX or sx
@media (max-width: Xpx) strings
boxShadow on the card
MUI <Card> component
textTransform: 'uppercase' on button labels (rely on CommonSubmitButton default or write literal uppercase strings)
inline SVG for icons (import from @mui/icons-material)
fontSize: '16px' or numeric fontSize on icons
bonus: Empty state uses the pattern from examples/ExampleEmptyState.tsx
A11y: delete icon has an aria-label that includes the key's label

tests.prompt: Generate a modal dialog for inviting a teammate to the workspace. Fields: email (required, must be valid email), role (dropdown with Admin / Editor / Viewer), optional personal message (textarea). Footer has Cancel + Send Invite buttons. Use Formik + Yup. Match the EyeLevel design system.

must_use: MUI <Dialog> with PaperProps={{ sx: { borderRadius: BORDER_RADIUS_CARD } }}
DialogTitle with a close IconButton (aria-label='close') as shown in examples/ExampleFormModal.tsx
CommonTextField for email and message
CommonCancelButton + CommonSubmitButton (in that order) in DialogActions
Formik + Yup schema with email().required() and role oneOf validation
fullWidth maxWidth='sm' on the Dialog
Named tokens from '@/constants' only
must_not_use: MUI <Button> directly for Submit
Hex literals
Hardcoded borderRadius px values
Cancel button with any accent color (must be grey/text)
Two CommonSubmitButtons (Cancel must use CommonCancelButton)
bonus: Multiline TextField for the personal message
Role Select uses MUI <Select> with consistent styling
isSubmitting disables the submit button without extra styling

tests.prompt: Build a Billing & Plan settings page. Show: (1) current plan card with plan name, usage bars (pages ingested, queries this month) and an 'Upgrade to Premium' CTA; (2) a payment method section with the current card on file and an 'Update payment method' button; (3) an invoice history table with Date, Amount, Status (paid/failed/pending as pills), Download link. Use the design system.

must_use: GxUsageCard or a close analog for the plan usage section
gx-premium-button variant for the Upgrade CTA
GxPill with variant='success'|'warning'|'error' for invoice status
GxCard for each top-level section with Stack spacing={3} between them
GxSectionHeader for 'PAYMENT METHOD' and 'INVOICE HISTORY'
Responsive content padding: MAIN_CONTENT_PADDING at md+, smaller at xs
Icons from @mui/icons-material (DownloadIcon for invoice download)
must_not_use: Hex literals
MUI <Card>
Inline border: '1px solid #... ' in sx
A button styled into a status pill (use GxPill)
Elevation / boxShadow on any surface
bonus: Failed invoices use ERROR_RED via GxPill variant='error'
Download icon is a properly labeled IconButton in the table row
Empty state if no invoices yet

tests.prompt: Build a public-facing marketing landing page for a brand-new EyeLevel product (no dashboard chrome). The page is a single React component composing four sections from top to bottom: (1) a header bar with the EyeLevel lockup on the left, three nav links (PRODUCT / DOCS / PRICING) in the center, and a 'BOOK A DEMO' CTA on the right; (2) a hero section on a navy canvas with a coral eyebrow 'NEW · FOR ENTERPRISES', an 800-weight white headline, a body sentence, and a primary CTA; (3) a feature grid section on a tint canvas with three GxCards in a row, each with a coral eyebrow + card heading + 2-sentence body; (4) a navy footer with the EyeLevel lockup (white variant), four columns of nav links under green eyebrow headings, and a copyright row. Use the design system. The page must be standalone — imports resolve to the project's own src/, never to groundx-ai-dashboard.

must_use: Composes four section components (HeaderBar, HeroSection, FeatureGrid, SiteFooter) — the public-page layout vocabulary from references/layout.md § Public-page layout
GxCard for each card-shaped surface (the three feature tiles)
CommonSubmitButton for the 'BOOK A DEMO' CTA

Two logo variants used per surface luminance — eyelevel-logo-color.png on the white header bar, eyelevel-logo-white.png on the navy footer (literal paths to ../eyelevel-design-standards/assets/logos/)

Eyebrow color swap per surface: coral on light surfaces (the feature-grid eyebrows), green on dark surfaces (the footer column eyebrows). Use surface-aware tokens or correct named colors from @/constants
Named tokens from '@/constants' only — NAVY, GREEN, CORAL, TINT, BORDER, BORDER_RADIUS_CARD, etc.
Sections are full-bleed with inner content constrained to a maxWidth (~1280–1440px) and centered horizontally
Responsive behavior — feature grid drops from repeat(3,1fr) to 1fr at xs via sx responsive object syntax
The 'NEW · FOR ENTERPRISES' eyebrow is written as a literal uppercase string with a U+00B7 middle dot — no textTransform
must_not_use: Any import from 'groundx-ai-dashboard' (the page must be standalone — imports resolve to the project's own src/)
Hex literals anywhere in JSX or sx
@media (max-width: Xpx) strings (use theme.breakpoints or sx responsive objects)
boxShadow on any surface (header, hero, cards, footer)
MUI <Card> (use GxCard)
MUI <Button> directly for the CTA (use CommonSubmitButton)
textTransform: 'uppercase' on any label (literal uppercase strings in JSX)
A photographic background on the hero (default to solid navy unless the prompt asks for imagery)
elevation prop on the AppBar / header
bonus: Header uses 1px BORDER hairline along the bottom edge (no shadow)
Hero CTA shows the coral-rest, green-on-hover convention via CommonSubmitButton without overriding it
Section components are factored to src/shared/components/ so they can drop into another project (HeroSection, FeatureGrid, SiteFooter) without rewrites
Voice register matches voice.md — no 'unleash', 'next-generation', 'cutting-edge', or unsupported superlatives in the headline / body

sanity_checks.description: Files that must exist for the skill and its dependency to render correctly.

how_to_run: For each test prompt, run the agent in a session with both skills enabled, save the generated code, then grep for forbidden patterns: (a) '#' followed by a hex character inside .tsx files, (b) '@media (max-width', (c) 'boxShadow', (d) 'fontSize: "' on icons, (e) literal '<Card>' imports from '@mui/material'. Each hit fails a must_not_use criterion. Then inspect imports to confirm the must_use criteria. The sanity_checks can be automated with: for file in required_files; do test -f "$SKILL_DIR/$file"; done.
